reasoning: GenDB achieves excellent performance at 55ms (1.56x faster than DuckDB's 86ms average), but a critical floating-point precision loss in parallel aggregation causes the N,O group sum_qty to deviate by 0.06% from expected values. This correctness issue must be fixed immediately to pass validation. Applying Kahan summation will resolve precision loss with minimal performance overhead while maintaining the current speed advantage.
focus_areas: Fix floating-point precision in parallel reduction using Kahan summation
Ensure reproducible numerical accuracy across all group aggregates
Maintain current parallelization and performance characteristics
strategy_notes: Apply Kahan summation (compensated summation) in the aggregation loop to eliminate floating-point rounding errors in parallel thread merges. This is a semantic fix in the cpu_bound/aggregation domain. The fix has medium risk but is critical for correctness. Once applied, GenDB should pass validation while maintaining its 1.56x speed advantage over DuckDB, triggering an early win. Implementation: Replace standard += operators for sum_qty, sum_base_price, sum_discount, sum_tax with Kahan summation formula that tracks a compensation variable. This adds ~10% overhead but is acceptable given the current 1.56x advantage.
notes: This is a critical correctness-first iteration. Performance optimization (SIMD, zone maps, hash tables) can follow after validation passes. The query is already winning on performance, so focus is entirely on fixing precision to achieve a complete success.
